articles.title: Bank Apps Showed Wrong Transactions
articles.summary: Some people using apps from Lloyds, Halifax, and Bank of Scotland saw other people's bank transactions on their phones. This was a mistake called a glitch. They could see things like payments from pubs or wages from jobs. One woman saw six different people's accounts, including their National Insurance numbers, which are important for benefits. The banks said sorry and fixed the problem. It is not clear how many people were affected. This has happened before with these banks. The apps had issues for a few hours in the morning, and a website called Downdetector.com showed many reports. People are worried because they saw private information like where others work or live.

articles.title: The Pussycat Dolls Get Back Together for Music and Shows
articles.summary: The Pussycat Dolls, a famous girl group, are reuniting after many years. Nicole Scherzinger, Ashley Roberts, and Kimberly Wyatt are the three members who will perform together again. They have made a new song called Club Song and will go on a big world tour starting in June. The tour will visit many places like North America, Europe, and the UK, including cities like London and Dublin. The group first became popular in 2005 with hits like Buttons. They are happy to be back and celebrate as women. Some other original members are not joining this time, but the group says their lineup can change. They are excited to sing and dance on stage again.

articles.title: Is Student Loan Repayment Fair for Graduates?
articles.summary: In England, some politicians are checking if student loans are fair for people who finished college. They are looking at a rule that says graduates must start paying back their loans when they earn over £29,385, and this amount won't go up with prices for a few years. This means graduates might pay more money back sooner. Many graduates are unhappy because they feel the rules changed and they didn't understand them well. A woman named Natalie says her loan grew from £52,000 to £75,500 even though she paid some back, and she thinks students weren't told clearly about the costs. The government says freezing the amount helps protect taxpayers and students. The inquiry will ask if this is unfair to younger people.

articles.title: U.S. Releases Oil to Help Lower Gas Prices
articles.summary: The United States is taking action to help people pay less for gas. The government will release 172 million barrels of oil from a special storage called the Strategic Petroleum Reserve. This oil is kept for emergencies. Energy Secretary Chris Wright announced this plan. The release will start next week and take about 120 days to finish. The U.S. has over 415 million barrels in the reserve right now. The goal is to lower oil prices, which have been going up because of a war in Iran. President Donald Trump said he will reduce the reserve a little and then fill it back up later. Some leaders, like Senator Chuck Schumer, think this move might not solve all the problems caused by the war. This is part of a bigger effort with other countries to make oil cheaper for everyone.

articles.title: Trump Team Tries New Way to Add Taxes on Imports After Court Said No
articles.summary: The Trump administration is starting a new process to try to put taxes on imports from other countries. This comes after the Supreme Court said no to their old taxes. They want to protect American jobs by making sure foreign companies don't have an unfair advantage. The investigation will look at countries like China, Mexico, and Japan to see if they have too many goods coming into the U.S. or if they help their companies too much. The goal is to replace the old taxes that were stopped by the court. This could affect trade deals and the economy, and it's happening during elections and other world events.

articles.title: Iran Blocks Oil Ships in Important Waterway
articles.summary: Iran has attacked ships in the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway between Iran and the Arabian Peninsula that is very important for oil shipping. This happened after the United States and Israel attacked Iranian targets. Iran says it will stop oil from passing through the strait to the U.S. and its allies. The attacks have made many countries worried because they need oil from this area. Countries are trying to keep oil flowing, but the situation is tense. This story shows how conflicts can affect global energy supplies and cause fear around the world.
